content.text:
Now I have a clear view of the molecule with all atom indices labeled. Let me examine atom C:33 (carbon with index 33) more carefully to identify its neighbor atoms.

From the image, I can see atom C:33 is located in the left portion of the molecule. Looking at the bonds connected to C:33, I can clearly identify:

- C:32 (carbon with index 32) - directly bonded to C:33 (to the right)
- C:35 (carbon with index 35) - directly bonded to C:33 (to the upper left)
- H:39 (hydrogen with index 39) - directly bonded to C:33 (to the lower left)

I can confirm these connections by observing the molecular structure. C:33 is part of a ring system, and it has three covalent bonds: two to carbon atoms (C:32 and C:35) and one to a hydrogen atom (H:39).

The geomconf_index for this molecule appears to be 1603, as indicated by the file name "geomconf_1603.xyz".

Therefore, the neighbor atoms of atom C:33 are:
- Atom index 32, element C
- Atom index 35, element C  
- Atom index 39, element H

Geomconf_index: 1603

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 33, whose element should be C. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
